operating_state: Net sales declined 16% to $12.8 billion in Q2 driven by elevated interest rates, economic uncertainties, and high used inventory levels across equipment segments. Production and precision agriculture sales fell 21% from reduced U.S. demand while construction and forestry dropped 23% due to lower volumes in U.S. and Brazil. Small agriculture and turf declined 6% with U.S. and Europe weakness partially offset by India growth. Financial services generated $391 million net income versus $370 million prior year.
cost_structure: Cost of sales ratio increased to 68.1% from production inefficiencies at lower volumes and incremental tariff costs of approximately $95 million, partially offset by reduced material costs and lower employee profit-sharing incentives. Operating margins compressed across equipment segments with production and precision agriculture declining to 22.0% from 25.1% and construction and forestry falling to 12.9% from 17.4%. Research and development expenses remained stable while selling and administrative expenses declined.
material_events: Completed Banco John Deere S.A. joint venture in Brazil through sale of 50% ownership to Banco Bradesco in February 2025, resulting in deconsolidation and $362 million equity method investment. Executed $838 million in share repurchases year-to-date under $18 billion authorization program with $8.2 billion remaining available. Increased quarterly dividend to $1.62 per share from $1.47 prior year.
macro_exposures: New tariffs imposed in Q2 2025 resulted in approximately $95 million direct incremental costs with ongoing uncertainty from evolving trade policies potentially affecting supply chains and export competitiveness. Federal Trade Commission filed antitrust lawsuit in January 2025 alleging monopolization in repair markets, seeking permanent injunction for equipment access by owners and independent repair providers. High interest rates continue pressuring customer demand and financing spreads while foreign exchange translation provided $300 million positive impact year-to-date.
forward_outlook: Management expects agriculture and turf equipment sales volumes to remain lower than prior year for remainder of 2025 due to reduced demand from high interest rates and elevated used inventory levels. Construction equipment industry sales forecasted down in U.S. and Canada from moderating housing starts and softening rental markets. Financial services net income expected up with favorable selling expenses offsetting unfavorable financing spreads.
uncertainty_profile: FTC antitrust litigation represents primary near-term uncertainty with potential for permanent injunctive relief requiring expanded access to repair tools and resources for independent providers, materially impacting service business model and competitive positioning. Variable interest entity structure for Brazilian joint venture creates ongoing funding and guarantee obligations with maximum exposure. Elevated credit losses in agriculture and turf financing portfolio due to deteriorating market conditions require intensified monitoring.
